channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Aftershock from a political 'earthquake' as Le Pen barred from presidential run in 2027
item.description: Le Pen called her ban from running a "nuclear bomb" by the system, after a court convicted her of embezzlement and blocked her from office for five years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fourth US soldier found dead after training accident in Lithuania
item.description: The U.S. military says the final U.S. soldier who went missing in Lithuania has been found dead, ending a massive weeklong search for the four service members whose armored vehicle was pulled from a swampy training area.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fourth missing US soldier in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Iran mulls preemptive strike on US base after Trump bomb threats
item.description: Iran is considering its options in deterring President Trump amid his repeated threats of direct military engagement by possibly launching its own strike on a U.S. base in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE admits 'administrative error' in deporting Maryland man to El Salvador
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has admitted to mistakenly deporting a Maryland man with protected legal status to a notorious El Salvador prison.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At just 29, Jordan Bardella inherits the French far-right spotlight, whether he’s ready or not
item.description: He wears his suits like armor, smiles like a pop star and has more than 2 million followers on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EU prepared to retaliate against Trump tariffs, von der Leyen says
item.description: The European Union (EU) is prepared to retaliate against President Trump’s existing and upcoming tariffs, although the trade bloc is open to negotiations with Washington, according to European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Here's a look at the election ban on France's far-right Le Pen and the legal issues
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French far-right leader Marine Le Pen has denounced as “a democratic scandal” the court ruling that placed a five-year ban on her seeking public office for embezzling European Union funds. The Paris court says it would be undemocratic to allow a convicted official to run for president.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope's work during convalescence includes clearing path for saints from Venezuela, Papua New Guinea
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis spends part of his days at his desk working and concelebrates Mass daily in his private chapel, the Vatican said Tuesday, in signs of continued improvements during his convalescence at the Vatican after beating life-threatening double pneumonia during a five-week hos
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany says US talks on Russia-Ukraine war are 'deadlocked,' but China has different take
item.description: German Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock says U.S. talks on Russia-Ukraine war are in a "deadlock," while Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi says the parties are making progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyprus court drops all rape charges against 5 Israelis after doubting British woman's credibility
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — A criminal court in Cyprus has acquitted and dropped all charges against five Israelis accused of gang-raping a British woman in a hotel room after ruling her testimony as not credible and replete with “fundamental contradictions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A fireball from a burst gas pipeline in Malaysia injures 145 people
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — A fireball that erupted from a burst gas pipeline soared into the sky outside Malaysia's largest city and injured 145 people as it burned for several hours before being put out, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Germany sees Ukraine truce efforts as deadlocked, while China says the talks are encouraging
item.description: Germany’s chief diplomat is describing U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to secure a truce in the three-year war between Russia and Ukraine as deadlocked.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela will have its first female saint after approval from Pope Francis
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela is set to have its first female saint following approval from Pope Francis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Finnish president: Trump should give Putin 3 weeks to agree Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: Finnish President Alexander Stubb told Fox News that President Trump should impose an April 20 deadline for his Russian counterpart, Vladimir Putin, to agree a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenland's new prime minister: U.S. 'will not get' island
item.description: Greenland's new Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen has called out President Trump's claims that the U.S. will "get Greenland 100 percent" and has reiterated that Greenland will decide its own future and will not be shaken by Trump's remarks.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Red Cross: Israel killed 8 Palestinian medics in strikes on ambulances
item.description: The Red Cross said Israel killed eight Palestinian medics when it struck ambulances in southern Gaza. United Nations officials said the Red Cross workers' bodies, along with other Palestinian rescue workers, were recovered from a grave in the sand, Reuters reported.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: A Kremlin official says Russia sees efforts to end Ukraine war as a drawn-out process
item.description: Russia views efforts to end its three-year war with Ukraine as “a drawn-out process,” a Kremlin spokesman said Monday, after U.S. President Donald Trump expressed frustration with the two countries’ leaders as he tries to bring about a truce.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Echoing Hamas, Erdogan reportedly calls for Israel's destruction amid push to rekindle ties with Trump
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan reportedly called for Israel's destruction during Eid prayers, drawing fierce condemnation from Israel, amid rising regional tensions over Gaza and Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year
item.description: Pakistan plans to expel 3 million Afghans from the country this year, as a deadline for them to voluntarily leave the capital and surrounding areas expired on Monday.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN and RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chair of African charity Prince Harry co-founded says the royal orchestrated a bullying campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The chairperson of an African charity co-founded by Prince Harry accused the royal on Sunday of orchestrating a bullying and harassment campaign to try to force her out as she pushed back following his abrupt resignation from the organization.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Smell of death permeates Myanmar cities after quake kills over 1,600, leaves others buried
item.description: The 7.7 magnitude quake hit midday Friday with an epicenter near Mandalay, bringing down scores of buildings and damaging other infrastructure like the city’s airport.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with tariffs, says he is 'pissed off' at Putin
item.description: President Trump also threatened major new sanctions against Russia if he believes it is Vladimir Putin's fault for failing to reach a peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Russian attack on hospital, shopping center leaves 2 dead as Ukraine braces for fresh offensive
item.description: A Russian drone attack killed two people in Kharkiv on Sunday amid a fresh offensive ahead of peace talks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Landmark UK report on Hamas massacre exposes worst attack on Jews since Holocaust
item.description: A UK Parliament report chaired by respected historian Lord Andrew Roberts calls the Oct. 7 Hamas attack in Israel deliberate and brutal, urging democracies to combat denial and misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth announces plan for US command upgrades in Japan to deter China
item.description: U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth announced a plan to upgrade the U.S. military command in Japan, an "indispensable" country against Chinese aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With federal election looming, Liberal Canadian PM accused of plagiarism while studying at Oxford: report
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is embroiled in controversy after a Canadian news outlet discovered at least 10 instances of possible plagiarism in his 1995 Oxford thesis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Countries sending humanitarian aid after Myanmar earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll from Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday that 1,644 people have been killed, with thousands of others injured and dozens missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Countries that have issued travel advisories for US
item.description: Denmark, Finland, France and Germany noted the U.S. requires individuals entering to choose "male" or "female" on ESTA and visa applications.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Bali expands tourist guidelines over misbehavior from travelers
item.description: Rules include shelling out Bali's tourist levy — equivalent to $9 USD — and wearing "modest and respectful clothing" in public.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation
